judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with comparable depth; Report 2 adds extra detail (missing checkout steps, privilege-leak framing of the OIDC issue) but both fully satisfy the graded criteria.

report_1.evidence: AUTH_RELAXED = "true" ... Relaxed authentication in production is a critical security hole. Set to false. PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS ... a pre-provisioned QA super-admin account is a backdoor. SHADOW_SCORING_SINK ... Critically, config.py:18-22 record_shadow_observation writes to the sink whenever a sink URI is set, regardless of SHADOW_SCORING_ENABLED. LEGACY_BILLING_SECRET_ARN ... Confirm and set the prod billing secret ARN.

report_2.evidence: AUTH_RELAXED = "true" - relaxed authentication in production ... PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS ... a preset test account granted superadmin ... SHADOW_SCORING_SINK ... shadow scoring is effectively armed in prod, and it would write production observation records into the staging S3 bucket. LEGACY_BILLING_SECRET_ARN ... a dead-fossil secret injection from a prior architecture

report_1.evidence: So the API base URL in the shipped production bundle is https://staging-api.example.com -- the production frontend would call the staging API. There is no .env.production file in the repo ... To fix: add frontend/.env.production with the prod API URL and build prod with --mode production

report_2.evidence: Result: the production bundle ships with https://staging-api.example.com baked in, so real production users' browsers would call the staging API ... Fix = add frontend/.env.production with the prod URL and build the prod-destined artifact with --mode production (a separate build, not a promoted staging digest)

report_1.evidence: The runbook says migrations auto-apply during deploy, but nothing here creates/points at a prod Postgres, and the migrations themselves (0001-0003) are empty stubs. The prod DB and its connection config must exist for a first launch and are not visible in this config.

report_2.evidence: No migration step in the pipeline. docs/prod-runbook.md:6 claims "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but there is no such step; migrations 0001-0003 exist. For the first launch the prod schema won't be created by the pipeline -- it must be run as a manual first pass

report_1.evidence: the prod deploy job runs as environment: production (deploy.yml:32), so its OIDC subject is ...:environment:production, which will not match this staging trust condition. Change staging -> production.

report_2.evidence: Fix: set prod's oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production" -- its own scoped trust condition, per project documentation (a stricter env needs its own, never an inherited/shared one).
